Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Cambum Business Group, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jul-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Attorney, John A. Kithas, Esq. (415-788-8100) appears for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Jennifer E. Elgie of Lewis Brisbois et al (415-438-6610), appears for defendant/opposing party, Starwood Hotels & Resorts

Attorney, Maria A. Caruana of John A. Biard Law Offices (925-945-4492), appears telephonically for defendant/opposing party, Cambium Business Group

The cause is argued and submitted by all sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with additional orders as follows. PLAINTIFF YICHENG (THOMAS) ZHANG'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Cambum Business Group, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories and for sanctions is granted in part. With two exceptions, the court adopts the recommended rulings of the judge pro tem and the reasons for those rulings. The first exception is that Defendant Cambium Business Group should be required to provide a supplemental response to interrogatory 6 that not only identifies any treatises, manuals, promotional materials, catalogues and guides that it referenced in connection with its work on the desk, but must also identify all such materials that it consulted in any way in connection with its work on the desk. No further response is required for interrogatory 9. Cambium must provide a further response to interrogatory 18 as recommended by the judge pro tem. The second exception is that Cambium is required to pay sanctions of $1000, not $500. Supplemental responses and payment are due by July 29, 2016. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)
